repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 — non-Gaussian cumulant direction recovery, not SCORE score-Jacobian geometry

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score — establishes the SCORE leaf characterization; the mirror two-point instantiation is the added specialization
rolland2022score and chernoff1952measure do not state this score-gap/Chernoff expansion, but the fixed-path Taylor calculation is a narrow specialization of their machinery
chernoff1952measure supplies the simple-versus-simple exponent; conditional on Theorem 1, its application to this two-point experiment is direct
hoyer2008nonlinear — the linear-Gaussian additive model is the reversible exceptional boundary
koehler2023efficiency studies score-matching versus likelihood for density-estimation parameters, not causal-order error exponents

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1, Theorem 2, and Open-ended Question 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses the closer score-based causal-discovery literature—Montagna et al., “Shortcuts for causal discovery of nonlinear models by score matching” (2023 preprint) and Montagna et al., “Score matching through the roof: linear, nonlinear, and latent variables causal discovery” (CLeaR 2025)—both of which bear on bivariate nonlinear score-based ordering and must be distinguished from the proposed local efficiency frontier.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Chernoff exponent is claimed only for the explicitly constructed two-node mirror Gaussian-ANM subexperiment, not for a global nonlinear-ANM minimax class.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Exact likelihood order-testing frontier conditional on the mirror-symmetric subexperiment and its stated regularity conditions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / ass:jacobian-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Any feasible-score conclusion is conditional on cross-fitted L4 estimability and on r_n=o(C_m(delta_n)); the core does not claim this rate for neural-score implementations or all ANM laws.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The question asks only whether the specified detector attains the likelihood exponent under the declared relative nuisance-rate premise.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add and accurately position the two missed Montagna score-ordering papers; state whether their bivariate score-sortability and broader score-matching frameworks overlap the mirror path, and isolate the Chernoff-gap map as the strict remaining distinction.

Sharpen the feasible rung into a necessary-and-sufficient condition, or exhibit a concrete finite-dimensional score sieve attaining r_n=o(C_m(delta_n)) and state its resulting detectable local regime.

Lift the mirror pair to a nondegenerate Hermite-tangent class with uniform constants, or prove that no broader class can be indexed by C_m alone; this would turn the fixed-path calculation into a stronger field contribution.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision: incorporate the missed score-based causal-discovery comparators and state a precise non-overlap or strict local-efficiency advance before advancing to Stage 0.
